philosophy: The redesigned Contact custom-field taxonomy is organized around the single most important clinical and business dimension at MWC: whether a consultation is NEW or RENEWAL. This Consultation backbone (split into New vs Renewal) becomes the primary organizing structure, with supporting folders containing durable contact properties (Identity, Consent & Compliance, Source & Attribution), operational metadata (Operational Context, Experience & Feedback), and clinical context for new intakes. Deal/financial fields move to Opportunity per the refactor mandate; clinical intake data is flagged for EMR as the long-term system of record. This design eliminates vague catch-all folders (Additional Info, Contact, General Info), consolidates fragmented data domains (SOURCE split across folders; consent fields scattered; sales/deal fields in Consultation Record AND Additional Info), and provides intuitive, semantically coherent folders for PCC staff navigating the Contact record during both new and renewal patient interactions.

proposed_folders.name: Consultation — New
proposed_folders.purpose: Medical intake and chief complaint for NEW consultations: presenting symptoms, duration, prior treatments, diagnoses, medications, allergies, lifestyle factors (tobacco, alcohol), physical/sexual/psychologic review, emergency contact, primary care provider, and all authorizations/signatures. Represents the complete intake form response and clinical context captured at the first visit. Most high-usage fields; core new-patient workflow.

fields.name: Have you ever been diagnosed with any of the following? (Check all that apply)
What is the primary reason for today’s visit?
How long have these symptoms been present?
Do you currently smoke or use tobacco products?
Have you ever used testosterone or other hormone therapy?
May we contact your primary care provider regarding your care?
I acknowledge that I have received and understand the Notice of Privacy Practices.
I understand that my care may be provided in person and, when clinically appropriate, via telemedicine.
I confirm the information I provided is accurate to the best of my knowledge.
I authorize Men’s Wellness Centers and its licensed medical providers to evaluate, diagnose, and treat me, including laboratory testing when medically appropriate.
Do you have any known allergies (medications, foods, latex, or environmental)?
Current medications (include name, dose, and how often you take them)
What's the main reason you booked today?
Do you smoke or use tobacco?
Have you ever used testosterone or other hormone therapy?
If yes, please provide brief details (optional)
May we contact them about your care
How Long Has This Been Going On?

proposed_folders.name: Consultation — Renewal
proposed_folders.purpose: Forward-looking folder for renewal/ongoing-care consultation context. Reserved for future population as renewal workflow matures. Will capture: renewal reason, prior treatment efficacy, changes since last visit, continuation of care, renewal-specific authorizations and provider recommendations.

proposed_folders.name: Identity & Demographics
proposed_folders.purpose: Stable core identity information: date of birth, occupation, location/clinic assignment, and preferred location. Durable properties independent of consultation type; define who the patient is.

proposed_folders.name: Source & Attribution
proposed_folders.purpose: Lead source and marketing attribution: UTM parameters (source/medium/campaign/content/term), source URL, how the patient found MWC, referrer details, social media channel, and lead date. Consolidated from scattered SOURCE folder and intake-form source fields. Enables ROI/attribution analysis.

fields.name: How did you hear about Men’s Wellness Centers?

proposed_folders.name: Consent & Compliance
proposed_folders.purpose: Legal consent tracking and privacy compliance: consent date/time/method/source/IP, communication preference, SMS opt-in status. Controls patient contact authorization and regulatory compliance. Consolidated from scattered Contact/Additional Info consent fields.

proposed_folders.name: Operational Context
proposed_folders.purpose: Call-center and internal operations: contact disposition (Scheduled, No-Show, Declined, etc.), last contact channel and date, patient advisor assignment, session tracking, and transactional metadata. Separate from consultation data; supports workflow routing and historical audit.

proposed_folders.name: Experience & Feedback
proposed_folders.purpose: Post-visit patient satisfaction and feedback: overall experience rating, staff interaction rating, referral likelihood (NPS), and optional open feedback. Lightweight feedback fields supporting quality improvement and retention insights. Kept on Contact (not moved) as these are non-clinical, summary-level feedback.

fields.name: How would you rate your overall experience today?
How would you rate your interaction with our staff?
Would you recommend us to a friend or family member?

move_off_contact.name: Past Surgery
move_off_contact.why: Clinical intake field (Past Surgery). Surgical history is part of medical record; EMR is long-term system of record. Low usage (6, cleanup).

move_off_contact.name: Sale Outcome
move_off_contact.why: Deal disposition field (Sale Outcome). Defines deal outcome (Sale, No-Sale, etc.). Per refactor, dispositions live on Opportunity. High usage (1147).

move_off_contact.name: Provider Making Recommendation
move_off_contact.why: Deal/recommendation field (Provider Making Recommendation). Provider who recommended/closed the sale is a deal attribute. High usage (1144).

move_off_contact.name: Total Program Amount
move_off_contact.why: Deal/financial field (Total Program Amount). Total deal value; belongs on Opportunity. High usage (1131).

move_off_contact.name: Price – Item 1
move_off_contact.why: Deal/financial field (Price – Item 1). Per refactor constraint, all sale/money/deal fields move to Opportunity. High usage (629) indicates Opportunity is primary system of record for pricing.

move_off_contact.name: Term Length – Item 1
move_off_contact.why: Deal/contract field (Term Length – Item 1). Contract/subscription term is deal data. High usage (626).

move_off_contact.name: Pay Type
move_off_contact.why: Deal/payment field (Pay Type). Payment method/type is a deal attribute. High usage (624).

move_off_contact.name: Product Sold - Item 1
move_off_contact.why: Deal/financial field (Product Sold - Item 1). Defines what product was sold; belongs on Opportunity. High usage (608).

move_off_contact.name: Consultation Fee
move_off_contact.why: Deal/financial field (Consultation Fee). Fee for service is a deal component. High usage (556).

move_off_contact.name: A&D Reason
move_off_contact.why: Deal disposition field (A&D Reason). No-Sale reason (Ask/Decline reason); belongs on Opportunity with sale_outcome. High usage (471).

move_off_contact.name: A&D Other Explanation
move_off_contact.why: Deal disposition field (A&D Other Explanation). Details on no-sale reason; move to Opportunity. High usage (128).

move_off_contact.name: Sale Type
move_off_contact.why: Deal field (Sale Type). New vs Renewal sale type; canonical opportunity.sale_type already exists on Opportunity. High usage (35).

move_off_contact.name: Product Sold – Item 2
move_off_contact.why: Deal/financial field (Product Sold – Item 2). Secondary product variant; move to Opportunity with Item 1.

move_off_contact.name: Term Length – Item 2
move_off_contact.why: Deal/contract field (Term Length – Item 2). Secondary term variant; move to Opportunity with Item 1.

move_off_contact.name: Price – Item 2
move_off_contact.why: Deal/financial field (Price – Item 2). Secondary pricing variant; move to Opportunity with Item 1 for deal consolidation.

move_off_contact.name: Print & Local
move_off_contact.why: Low usage (12, cleanup). Print/local marketing source unclear; consolidated into Source & Attribution via primary source fields. Retire.

move_off_contact.name: Survey — Open Feedback
move_off_contact.why: Low usage (11, cleanup). Unstructured open feedback; consolidated NPS/feedback captured in Experience folder. Retire.

move_off_contact.name: Cancellation Reason
move_off_contact.why: Low usage (9, cleanup). Appointment cancellation reason has minimal usage; operational context fields cover contact tracking. Retire.

move_off_contact.name: Referrer's Email Address
move_off_contact.why: Low usage (8, cleanup). Referrer email should be on a Referrer/Partner Contact record, not main patient contact. Retire.

move_off_contact.name: Preferred Appointment Date
move_off_contact.why: Low usage (8, cleanup). Appointment scheduling is native GHL Calendar function; redundant on Contact. Retire.

move_off_contact.name: Preferred Appointment Location
move_off_contact.why: Low usage (7, cleanup). Superseded by contact.preferred_location in Identity & Demographics folder. Retire.

move_off_contact.name: Ambassador Slug
move_off_contact.why: Low usage (4, cleanup). Ambassador program tracking unclear; minimal operational utility. Retire.

move_off_contact.name: Preferred Appointment Time
move_off_contact.why: Low usage (4, cleanup). Appointment scheduling is native GHL Calendar function; redundant on Contact. Retire.

move_off_contact.name: Service interested in?
move_off_contact.why: Low usage (4, cleanup). Service interest is captured in primary concern or product fields. Retire.

move_off_contact.name: Which Location Are You Interested In?
move_off_contact.why: Low usage (3, cleanup). Location preference captured via contact.preferred_location. Retire.

move_off_contact.name: Referred by Ambassador
move_off_contact.why: Low usage (1, cleanup). Ambassador referral tracking is sparse; consolidate or retire. Retire.

move_off_contact.name: chatbot_intent
move_off_contact.why: Zero usage (archive). Defunct BotFanatics integration; retire.

move_off_contact.name: SMS Opt-In Source
move_off_contact.why: Zero usage (archive). Redundant SMS tracking; sms_optin_date and sms_double_optin cover consent. Retire.
